title: "cancelled by user" reason string reads from wrong perspective on peer
description:
commands.rs:1031 — PaymentAction::Cancelled { reason: "cancelled by user" } is displayed on the peer side as "[pay] Session X cancelled by peer: cancelled by user". The phrase "by user" is the sender's self-reference; reading it from the peer's perspective it is ambiguous ("cancelled because of the user?" "which user?").

**Why:** The reason string was written from the canceller's perspective without considering the recipient's display context.
**How to apply:** Use a neutral reason like "user request" or "payer cancelled" that reads correctly on both sides.
close_reason: Changed reason string from 'cancelled by user' to 'payer cancelled' — reads correctly from recipient's perspective.
